title: Criticality in the disordered $N$-color Ashkin-Teller model
abstract: The $N$-color Ashkin-Teller model corresponds to $N$ Ising models coupled by four-spin interactions. We consider the two-dimensional case in presence of quenched disorder and use scale invariant scattering theory to determine all the solutions of the exact renormalization group fixed points equations. The weak disorder sector is characterized by a solution that, for any fixed $N$ larger than 1, is a line of fixed points with Ising thermal exponents and continuously varying magnetic exponents. The number of fixed point solutions allowed by the symmetries of the model increases at strong disorder illustrating the growing dependence on the distributions of the two random couplings. The presence of some critical exponents which do not depend on the symmetry parameter $N$ confirms this type of superuniversality as a peculiar feature of random criticality.
summary: This theoretical work analyzes the critical behavior of the disordered N-color Ashkin-Teller model using advanced scattering theory and the replica method. It determines the exact RG fixed points, revealing that the weak disorder regime supports a continuous line of fixed points. This demonstrates a form of superuniversality where critical exponents are robust against changes in the system's symmetry parameter N.
main_problem: The paper aims to determine all solutions to the exact renormalization group (RG) fixed point equations for the disordered N-color Ashkin-Teller model.
main_result: The weak disorder sector exhibits a line of fixed points with Ising thermal exponents and continuously varying magnetic exponents for N>1, indicating superuniversality in random criticality.
method: The methodology employs scale invariant scattering theory in two dimensions, utilizing the replica method to handle quenched disorder and solving crossing/unitarity equations derived from scattering amplitudes.
model_or_system: The system is the N-color Ashkin-Teller model, which couples N Ising models via four-spin interactions. The disorder is quenched, characterized by two independent random coupling distributions.
key_observables: Critical exponents (thermal and magnetic), scattering amplitudes ($S_i$), and the scaling dimension of the energy density field ($X_\epsilon$).
important_parameters: N (number of colors), disorder strength parameters ($|S_4|$ and $|S_7|$), and the replica number ($n 	o 0$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the scattering framework, conformal invariance, and the assumption of energy-independence of scattering amplitudes.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate elastic scattering amplitudes, RG flows for varying N, and phase diagrams for related random bond Ising models.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first defining the Hamiltonian and the RG framework, then analyzing the pure model fixed points, and finally extending the analysis to the disordered case using the replica method to find the continuous line of fixed points.
why_interesting: The concept of superuniversality, where critical exponents are independent of the symmetry parameter N despite the model depending on N, is a deep theoretical result relevant to understanding universality classes in complex interacting quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
